name: Fresh Lemon and Ginger Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Grease muffin cups with butter or line them with foil/paper.
3. Finely chop the ginger and grate the lemon peel to yield about 2 tablespoons.
4. In a large bowl, beat the butter and 1 cup sugar until pale and fluffy using a wooden spoon or electric mixer.
5. Beat in eggs one at a time.
6. Add the chopped ginger and grated lemon peel.
7. Stir baking soda into yogurt or buttermilk; it will bubble gently.
8. Gradually fold flour into the ginger mixture, alternating with the yogurt, until well combined.
9. Carefully scoop batter into prepared muffin cups.
10. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes, or until lightly browned and springy to the touch.
11. Remove muffins from the oven and let cool in the pan for 3 to 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.
12. Dip each muffin top and bottom into the mixture of lemon juice and sugar.
Note: If using a food processor, process ginger, lemon peel, and sugar until finely chopped (1-2 minutes). Add butter and pulse briefly. Incorporate eggs one at a time, then mix in baking soda, yogurt, and flour as directed above.
